_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave D1 (the EW-grade city: EM-152 city asset vocabulary, EM-153 deterministic CityGenerator, EM-154 instanced render path, EM-155 city snapshot contract, EM-156 old-town historic district, EM-157 instancing scoped to static sets)

resolution.summary: QE gate for the 6-item Wave D1 batch on build/wave-d1-ew-city (diff 4ae3755..HEAD): EM-152 (23 vendored CC0 GLBs under kenney-city/ + kaykit-city/, ~1.4 MB, CITY_MODEL_REGISTRY with all 23 frozen keys non-null, license rows both directions), EM-153 (cityLayout.ts pure deterministic Manhattan-grid generator — seeded block period 4-6 tiles, zoned blocks, road classification from actual neighbors, park guarantee, budget auto-shrink — zero Math.random/Date/module state, all variety via the repo's hashUnit idiom), EM-154 (CityScape.tsx: one raw InstancedMesh per piece-key x spatial-chunk x material-part, content-signature plan memo, StaticDrawUsage, per-chunk bounding spheres, ModelBoundary per piece key), EM-155 (city_seed: config -> WorldParams -> World -> to_snapshot/from_snapshot/world_state, int-coerced, default 1337, snapshot-authoritative over params on fork), EM-156 (deriveCoreRadius measured per snapshot from places + BAND_MAX + lane endpoints + zone discs; tile eligibility coreRadius + TILE guarantees zero intrusion; foliage/scenery wilderness clamped INSIDE the core disc), EM-157 (noopRaycast on every city mesh, unit-pinned). Full gates re-run by QE: backend 377/377, web 467/467 across 38 files, vite build clean. Adversarial probes: 7-test throwaway vitest suite on the REAL live /api/state snapshot (determinism incl. wire round-trip, 25-seed intrusion sweep at derived core radius 41.85 with zero hits, pre-Wave-C districtless/seedless snapshot, 10-seed instance budget incl. seed 0/negative/INT32_MAX), GLB magic-byte spot checks, free-scale grep over the full diff (zero LLM call sites), live browser pass (60 fps default AND zoomed, 0 console errors after fresh load + 11s settle, all 23 city GLBs fetched with zero boundary trips, click-to-focus transit on the castle works, city pieces non-interactive, lanes + villagers render). ONE probe family failed its console-clean half: with /models/** blocked the full Wave C procedural town renders perfectly (screenshot-verified, no blank canvas, no crash, ModelBoundary pins all fallbacks) BUT ~160 unhandled promise rejections from module-scope useGLTF.preload spill into the console, falsifying the preloadCityModels comment's claim that drei routes preload failures through an internal catch — recorded as the wave's one MAJOR issue. Zero blockers; gate is GREEN with that issue tracked for a cheap hardening pass.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against contracts/wave-d1.md by reading the full diff and probing, not by trusting agent reports. Rule 1 (free-scale law): grep over added lines of the full wave diff for chat/completion/anthropic/openai/gemini/ollama/provider/prompt call sites — zero production hits; the only matches are ledger/research docs and test_city_seed.py's MockProvider scaffolding; the only backend changes are city_seed plumbing (loader.py +3 sites, world.py +3 sites) plus the new test file. Rule 4 (additive-only): pre-W15 snapshot without city_seed restores 1337 (probed live-shape + backend test); world_state carries city_seed (GET /api/state on the running stack returned city_seed: 1337 alongside the 15 places). Rule 5 (CC0): 23/23 vendored files have ASSET_LICENSES.md rows; cityModels.test.ts enforces both directions incl. no-orphan sweeps; 3 GLBs spot-checked for glTF magic bytes (676c5446) — all valid. Rule 6 (no new runtime deps): all randomness via hashUnit('city:seed:gx:gz:purpose'); no seedrandom/Math.random/Date in cityLayout/CityScape; package.json untouched. Rule 7 (determinism): byte-identical JSON across parsed-twice AND a JSON wire round-trip of the REAL live snapshot; seed sensitivity confirmed; backend fork test pins snapshot-authoritative-over-params. Rule 8 (old-town invariant): 25-seed intrusion sweep — zero instances or block centers inside the derived core radius (41.85 for the live town); eligibility math (tile center > coreRadius + TILE) read and verified sound; Wave C town renders identically (live screenshots, click-to-focus works). Rule 9 (interactivity split): noopRaycast pinned by CityScape.test.tsx and present on the live meshes; clicking city set dressing focuses nothing. DOCKED ONE POINT on rule 10: with /models/** blocked (Playwright route abort + CDP cache disabled) the app renders the full Wave C procedural town flawlessly — but ~160 unhandled promise rejections ('Could not load /models/...: Failed to fetch') hit the console via pageerror, and the new preloadCityModels() comment in CozyWorld.tsx explicitly (and wrongly) claims this cannot happen. The render half of the invariant holds; the 'console clean of uncaught errors' half does not. Scale discipline verified by the suite's re-measured GLB footprints (roads exactly TILE 2.6).

test_coverage.notes: The wave ships 121 new tests (backend +10 test_city_seed.py: config wiring, malformed-params coercion, round-trip, string/dict coercion in from_snapshot, pre-W15 legacy snapshot, fork-with-edited-config, world_state + broadcast payload, yaml/embedded-mirror sync; web +111: cityModels.test.ts 56 incl. registry totality, GLB byte re-measurement, license rows both directions, payload budget; cityLayout.test.ts 19 incl. a full law pack across seeds — determinism, core clearance, totality, bounds, road connectivity, budget, districtless and seed-absent inputs; CityScape.test.tsx 14 incl. chunking math, registry-null skip, noopRaycast, render smoke; foliageLayout.test.ts wilderness-clamp additions). QE's hostile probes beyond the suites all passed: determinism on the REAL backend snapshot (suites use synthetic towns), wire round-trip identity, 25-seed intrusion (suite does fewer), hostile seeds (0, -5, 2147483647), degenerate 0/1-place worlds, blocks-outside-core. Docked one point: the fallback invariant's console-clean half has NO automated guard at any level — the Gate 2 blocked-models probe and the unit suite both only verify the render outcome, which is exactly how the unhandled-rejection spill shipped with a comment asserting the opposite; a Playwright-level pageerror assertion (or a preload rejection handler with a unit test) would have caught it. Honest residual gaps, none load-bearing: per-chunk frustum-culling efficacy and the contract's 'draw calls <= scene + 30' delta are unmeasurable without a no-city baseline (absolute measured numbers recorded instead); extractInstanceParts' merge fallback path (mismatched attributes) is unit-covered but no vendored asset currently exercises it live.

security.notes: No new endpoints, no new request shapes, no new agent-reachable text paths, zero new LLM surface (grep-verified over the full wave diff — the backend delta is one int config key threaded through existing snapshot plumbing). city_seed is defensively int-coerced at BOTH boundaries (World.__init__ try/except TypeError/ValueError, from_snapshot _int with default) — probed: string '7' coerces, dict falls back to 1337, never throws; a hostile snapshot value cannot crash restore or reach any eval/format surface (the seed only feeds hashUnit string keys client-side). The new external input class is 23 static CC0 GLB binaries (~1.4 MB), static-served, parsed client-side by GLTFLoader, license-audited file-by-file with two-direction registry coverage; magic bytes spot-verified. computeCityPlan consumes only places coordinates + the seed; all object key access is over the frozen CITY_PIECE_KEYS literal union — no model-authored strings enter the city path at all. No secrets, no auth, no SQL, no new file I/O.

regression_risk.notes: Full suites green (backend 377 — all 367 Wave C baseline tests intact; web 467 across 38 files — all 376 Wave C tests intact, foliageLayout retuned additively for the wilderness clamp) and the live Wave C town survived hands-on probing: lanes render, GLB buildings + villagers + critters present, click-to-focus transit eases to the castle, procedural fallback town renders perfectly under total asset blackout. Verified mitigations: SIZE stays 66 (the contract-blessed approach — WORLD_REACH 95.7 is a pure bounds constant, placeToWorld untouched, so every Wave C position law still pins); the wilderness clamp uses the SAME deriveCoreRadius the generator clears, so foliage and city can never overlap by construction; CityScape mounts outside the historic-core groups; plan memo is content-keyed so per-tick world churn never rebuilds GL buffers (read + verified). Watch items, none blocking: (1) docked point — the wave widens fog 70/160 -> 90/300, pushes the default camera to (46,38,46), maxDistance 100 -> 160, ground plane to WORLD_REACH-derived discs, and grows the shadow frustum to +/-64; all of it is eyeball-pinned constants, so a future retune can silently regress the Wave C framing with every test green; (2) a dev-session HMR edit of CityScape.tsx leaves stale-module cleanup errors that pin ModelBoundary fallbacks (city pieces vanish) until a full reload — observed in this QE session's console history from the build session, harmless in prod but confusing mid-development; (3) the empty road-framed blocks hugging the core (any block intersecting the core circle gets roads but no development) read as bald patches at the default seed — deterministic and contract-compliant, but a density pass may want partial-block development; (4) the 37 module-scope useGLTF.preload calls are now the single biggest console-noise risk on flaky networks (see the MAJOR issue).

frontend.changed_existing: foliageLayout.test.ts gained wilderness-clamp coverage (file now 22 tests); no existing assertion weakened — Wave C's 376 all still pass

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npx vitest run — 467/467 across 38 files; npm run build — clean in 8.49s (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only; the city GLB payload ships via public/, not the bundle)
test_results.qe_probes: 7 hostile vitest probes on the REAL live /api/state snapshot (throwaway, deleted — no generator bugs found): determinism parsed-twice + JSON wire round-trip byte-identical, seed sensitivity, 25-seed historic-core intrusion sweep (derived radius 41.85, zero instance or block hits), pre-Wave-C districtless+seedless snapshot === explicit-1337 plan, degenerate 0/1-place worlds, 10-seed instance budget incl. seeds 0/-5/2147483647. Static probes: free-scale grep over the full diff (zero LLM call sites), glTF magic bytes on 3 GLBs (676c5446), 23 vendored files == 23 license rows. Browser (live stack, web 5173 / backend 8000): GET /api/state carries city_seed 1337; fresh load + 11s settle = 0 console errors, 1 tolerated vite HMR WS warning (useSimulation.ts); all 23 city GLBs fetched, zero ModelBoundary trips; 60.2 fps default camera, 60.0-60.1 fps fully zoomed out (rAF over 3s), ~401 GL draw invocations/frame worst case (incl. shadow pass) via GL prototype patching; click-to-focus transit on the castle works; clicking city set dressing focuses nothing (noopRaycast also unit-pinned); blocked /models/** (route abort + CDP cache off): full Wave C procedural town renders, no blank canvas, no crash — but ~160 unhandled preload rejections (the MAJOR issue). Evidence: qe-wave-d1-default-camera.jpeg, qe-wave-d1-city-zoomed-fresh.jpeg, qe-wave-d1-focus-castle.jpeg, qe-wave-d1-blocked-models.jpeg (repo root).

issues.title: Blocked /models/** spills ~160 unhandled preload rejections — the rule-10 'console clean of uncaught errors' half fails, and the new comment claims otherwise
issues.detail: web/src/components/world3d/CozyWorld.tsx:61-71 — preloadCityModels() (23 new module-scope useGLTF.preload calls, joining Wave C's 14) wraps each call in try/catch and its comment asserts drei 'routes failures through suspend-react's internal catch... so a blocked /models/** never spills unhandled promise noise into the console (rule 10)'. Live probe (Playwright route.abort on **/models/** + CDP cache disabled, fresh load, 12s settle) falsifies this: try/catch only guards synchronous throws; the preload PROMISE rejections are unhandled, and ~160 pageerror events ('Error: Could not load /models/...: Failed to fetch') plus 77 browser net::ERR_FAILED resource logs hit the console. The invariant's render half holds completely — the full Wave C procedural town renders (qe-wave-d1-blocked-models.jpeg), every ModelBoundary pins its fallback, no blank canvas, no crash, sim fully usable — so this is console noise under an abnormal network state, not a functional break. This is Wave C MINOR issue 3 grown teeth: the wave tripled the preload count AND documented the wrong belief. Cheap fix: route preloads through a helper that attaches a .catch (e.g. preload via the loader promise where available) or a scoped unhandledrejection filter; add a pageerror assertion to the blocked-models probe so it can't regress silently.

issues.title: Empty road-framed blocks ring the historic core at the default seed
issues.detail: cityLayout.ts generate() develops a block only when EVERY tile is eligible, but emitRoads lays road tiles per-tile — so blocks that intersect the core circle (or the extent edge) get fully-framed roads around undeveloped grass. At the default town + seed 1337 this puts a visible band of empty blocks between the historic core and the developed ring (qe-wave-d1-city-zoomed-fresh.jpeg), with development concentrated toward the square's corners where full blocks fit the annulus. Deterministic, contract-compliant (clearance law trumps density), and arguably reads as 'outskirts' — but a future density/polish pass may want partial-block development (lot-level eligibility instead of block-level) or road pruning around undeveloped blocks.

issues.title: HMR edits to CityScape.tsx pin city pieces to absent until a full reload
issues.detail: Observed in this QE session's browser console history (stale module timestamps ?t=1781143145723 vs ?t=1781143146704): when vite hot-swaps CityScape, React runs the OLD module's effect cleanups; an earlier dev iteration's cleanup threw 'mesh.dispose is not a function' inside safelyCallDestroy, tripping ModelBoundary per piece and pinning fallback=null — the city silently vanishes for the rest of the session. HEAD's code has no cleanup (the in-code 'Gate 2 finding 1' note documents the fix) and a fresh load is clean (0 errors), so this is a dev-loop footgun, not a shipped defect: any FUTURE cleanup added to CityChunkMesh's effect re-opens it, and ModelBoundary has no retry path, so even a transient throw is permanent until reload.

issues.title: The contract's 'draw calls <= current scene + 30' delta is unverifiable live; absolute numbers recorded instead
issues.detail: There is no runtime toggle to render the scene without CityScape, so QE could not measure the Wave C baseline on the live stack and verify the +30 delta from contracts/wave-d1.md SD1d. Measured outcome instead: ~401 GL draw invocations/frame (drawElements* prototype patching, includes the shadow pass) fully zoomed out with the whole city in frustum, at a locked 60.0-60.1 fps; 60.2 fps at the default camera — the performance outcome the budget protects is comfortably met (gate: >=55 default / >=45 zoomed). The chunk x material-part fan-out (CHUNK_SPLIT_4/8) is the structural guard and is unit-tested; if a future wave wants the delta enforceable, add a ?nocity debug flag and assert in the perf probe.

issues.title: foliageLayout <-> cityLayout module cycle is load-order fragile by design
issues.detail: cityLayout.ts imports BAND_MAX from foliageLayout.ts while foliageLayout.ts imports deriveCoreRadius/townCenter from cityLayout.ts — a documented, currently-benign cycle (both sides dereference only inside function bodies). It held under vitest, vite dev, and vite build in this gate. Risk is latent: a future top-level use of either import (e.g. a module-scope precomputed constant) turns it into an undefined-at-evaluation bug that surfaces only at runtime. Cheapest structural fix when convenient: lift BAND_MAX (and similar shared constants) into worldSpace.ts or a constants module, breaking the cycle.

gate_decision.reason: All six Wave D1 items verified against contracts/wave-d1.md by direct diff reading plus hostile probing, and every hard gate is green: backend 377/377 ('377 passed, 1 warning in 27.79s'), web 467/467 across 38 files, npm run build clean, live stack at a locked 60 fps default AND zoomed (gate: >=55/>=45), fresh-load console clean (0 errors; only the tolerated useSimulation.ts vite HMR WS blip). The load-bearing invariants all hold under attack: EM-155 determinism is byte-identical on the REAL backend snapshot across parsed-twice and wire round-trip, with city_seed riding /api/state, snapshots, fork, and broadcast (backend-test-pinned, snapshot-authoritative over edited config); EM-156 holds at zero intrusions across a 25-seed sweep against the derived 41.85-unit core radius, and the eligibility math makes intrusion geometrically impossible; pre-Wave-C snapshots produce the default-1337 city; the instance budget holds across hostile seeds; all 23 assets are CC0-licensed both directions with valid GLB magic; the free-scale law holds (zero LLM call sites — the backend delta is one int through existing plumbing); EM-157 set dressing is pointer-invisible live and unit-pinned; Wave C regressions: none found (click-to-focus, lanes, villagers, procedural fallbacks all verified live). The single FAILED probe half — blocked /models/** spills ~160 unhandled preload rejections while the town itself degrades perfectly — is recorded as a MAJOR issue with a cheap, well-understood fix (attach rejection handlers to the 37 module-scope preloads; add a pageerror assertion to the probe), is only reachable under an abnormal network state, and does not meet the CRITICAL bar (no functional, data, or security impact). Gate rules: zero CRITICAL, contract 4 >= 3, security 5 >= 3. GREEN to proceed, with the preload-rejection hardening and the four MINORs handed to plan-intake.
